description: A healthy Chinese-American inspired dish that swaps traditional noodles for fresh zucchini spirals in a savory kung pao sauce with tender chicken, crunchy peanuts, and aromatic garlic and ginger - all under 300 calories per serving.
ingredients: 2 medium zucchini (about 8 oz each, ends trimmed)
6 oz skinless chicken breasts (cut into 1/2-inch pieces)
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper (to taste)
2 tbsp thinly sliced scallions along diagonal
1 1/2 tbsp reduced-sodium soy sauce (tamari for gluten-free)
1/2 tbsp Sambal Oelek Red Chili Paste (or more to taste)
instructions: Prepare zucchini noodles using a spiralizer with the thickest noodle blade, or a mandolin fitted with a julienne blade to cut the 2 medium zucchini into long spaghetti-like strips. Cut the strands into pieces about 6 to 8 inches long so they're easier to eat. Set aside on a clean towel or paper towels to absorb surface moisture.

Make the sauce by whisking together 1 1/2 tbsp reduced-sodium soy sauce, 1 tbsp balsamic vinegar, 1 tsp hoisin sauce, 2 1/2 tbsp water, 1/2 tbsp Sambal Oelek, 2 tsp sugar, and 2 tsp cornstarch in a small bowl until smooth and cornstarch is fully dissolved. Set aside.

Season 6 oz chicken pieces with salt and pepper to taste. Heat 1 teaspoon grapeseed or canola oil in a large, deep nonstick pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add the chicken in a single layer and cook until browned and cooked through (internal temp 165°F), about 4 to 5 minutes. Transfer to a plate and set aside.

Reduce heat to medium and add 1 teaspoon sesame oil, 2 cloves minced garlic, and 1 tsp ground ginger to the same skillet. Cook until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Add the 1/2 red bell pepper pieces and stir for 30 seconds.
Give the sauce a quick re-whisk, then pour into the pan. Bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer until thickened and bubbling, about 1-2 minutes.

Stir in the zucchini noodles and cook, tossing, for about 2 minutes until just tender and coated with sauce. If it seems dry, don't worry — the zucchini will release moisture which helps create a sauce. Mix in the reserved chicken and toss to combine. Divide between 2 bowls (about 2 cups each) and top with 2 tbsp crushed peanuts and 2 tbsp sliced scallions.
